_comment: EU acts by name -> CELEX, for the ⌘K resolver (lib/resolve.py) AND the citation engine (lib/lagrum.load_namedacts), which links 'artikel N i <name>' in running text the way it links named SFS laws. Hand-edited, and deliberately sparse: `abbr`, the acronym(s) people cite by (GDPR, NIS2, …), and `label`, the established short name(s). An act whose short title sits in the official title's trailing parenthesis (GDPR '(allmän dataskyddsförordning)', DSA '(förordningen om digitala tjänster)', Rom I '(Rom I)') ideally has it extracted from the title mechanically (a later step); until then list the definite/indefinite forms the engine should match under `label`. `abbr`/`label` may each be a string or a list; both are matched case-insensitively, and an `art N`/`artikel N`/`article N` tail becomes the `#N` fragment. Leading determiners/adjectives (den, EU:s, allmänna) are matched by the grammar, so `label` holds only the noun-phrase head ('dataskyddsförordningen', not 'den allmänna dataskyddsförordningen'). Sector-1 CELEX (the treaties + Charter, keyed by consolidated-text CELEX like '12016E/TXT') are the exception: lib/lagrum.load_treaties reads them separately and links them EVERYWHERE (not just where a caller opts into named acts), and their determiner-led forms ('EU:s rättighetsstadga') ARE listed since the treaty grammar matches the whole name.

label: stadgan
rättighetsstadgan
rättighetsstadga
EU:s rättighetsstadga
Europeiska unionens stadga om de grundläggande rättigheterna
EU:s stadga om de grundläggande rättigheterna
